mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses equity discount rates and pushes investors toward risk assets in search of yield, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during zero-rate periods (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) are not significantly higher than the full-sample average, or rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals accelerating labor market recovery, which raises earnings growth expectations and drives S&P 500 gains over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.5pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 14.7% peak in April 2020 to 6.7% by November 2020
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following qualifying UNRATE declines are not positive in at least 55% of triggered instances, or t-test p > 0.05 versus non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand recovery that jointly reinforce corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter that exceed what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM > 0 AND RSAFS MoM > 0 for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1543k units and RSAFS at $539,114M as of late 2020 vintages
failure_condition: The joint-trigger 60-day forward return distribution is not statistically distinguishable from the single-series HOUST-only or RSAFS-only trigger distributions (interaction term insignificant at p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounding sharply from a pandemic-induced trough signals reopening of supply chains and factory capacity, which in a post-shock recovery regime translates into higher forward earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 1% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 103.98 for 2020-11-01 reflecting continued rebound from COVID trough
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following qualifying INDPRO rebounds are not positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears outside the 2020-2021 recovery window when tested in later regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation accelerates above 2.5%, markets price in an earlier-than-expected withdrawal of monetary accommodation, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 260.9 (revised) as of 2020-11 vintage, well below the 2.5% YoY trigger threshold at decision time
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after CPI YoY crosses 2.5% are not significantly negative relative to baseline, or FEDFUNDS fails to rise within 6 months following the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive month-over-month nonfarm payroll growth reflects steady labor income generation that supports consumer spending and near-term equity sentiment, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week regardless of the broader macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,733k (revised) as of 2020-11 vintage, recovering from pandemic lows
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during periods of positive PAYEMS MoM growth are not significantly higher than during negative-growth periods (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in retail sales growth and payroll employment growth signals an emerging demand-labor feedback loop where weaker consumer spending reduces hiring, and weaker hiring further reduces spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month that exceed the effect of either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 0 AND PAYEMS MoM growth < 0 in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:both series showed volatile recovery paths through late 2020 amid resurgent COVID cases
regime_hint: fragile post-shock recovery with renewed contagion risk
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS/PAYEMS negative-trigger 20-day forward return is not more negative than either single-series negative trigger at p < 0.10, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while unemployment remains elevated above 6%, markets infer an extended period of accommodative policy since the Fed's dual mandate delays tightening until labor slack is absorbed, supporting a sustained positive S&P 500 drift over the following quarter that would not hold once labor slack closes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and UNRATE at 6.7% as of Nov 2020 vintage
regime_hint: zero lower bound with substantial labor market slack
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during the joint FEDFUNDS-near-zero and UNRATE-above-6% regime are not significantly positive versus baseline, or the effect reverses once UNRATE falls below 5% while rates remain near zero.
